title: The Mirror of the Blessed Life of Jesus Christ
summary: The checked-in XML is the exact pinned General Corpus copy of a 1997 University of Michigan electronic text keyed from Lawrence F. Powell's 1908 edition. Powell printed the complete Brasenose MS 9 beta-1 version of Nicholas Love's Middle English Mirror, including the Latin Attende lector witness paratext, sixty-three chapters in seven day-sections, the integrated Eucharistic treatise conventionally numbered chapter 64, and final prayer; he based the text on Brasenose MS 9 and collated Bodleian MS e. Musaeo 35 and the Sherard manuscript now Yale Beinecke MS 324. U-M retained selected editorial front matter and the Powell/Brasenose text through page 324, omitted Powell's word-list and nearly all manuscript marginalia, and adjusted several headings and subdivisions. The graph keeps Love's adapted Middle English work, its abstract pseudo-Bonaventuran Latin source, physical witnesses, print edition, digital encoding, repository artifact, facsimiles, supporting catalogs, access routes, and rights layers distinct.
review_notes: Completeness refers to the Powell/Brasenose beta-1 version, not to one universal final authorial form of a work transmitted in early beta and alpha revisions.

The Latin Attende lector printed on Powell page [6] and encoded at LoveMirrour:3 is medieval witness paratext; it is not Powell editorial prose even though it appears after his contents and inside XML FRONT.

Powell's 'MS. Brasenose College e. 9' is current Oxford, Brasenose College MS 9; his Sherard/Lord Aldenham collation witness is current Yale Beinecke MS 324, not the different Aldenham Mirror manuscript now Takamiya MS 63.
No complete public manuscript facsimile was verified for the three source witnesses. The only cached manuscript image is one archived sample of Bodleian MS e. Musaeo 35, page 80.

entities.label: General Corpus XML artifact LoveMirrour
entities.description: The exact checked-in DLPSTEXTCLASS XML file in the pinned CME submodule.

entities.label: U-M Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse electronic text LoveMirrour
alternate_labels: University of Michigan Humanities Text Initiative LoveMirrour
entities.description: A 1997 electronic text keyed by Sencor from Powell's 1908 edition, sampled at five percent and reported within one error per 20,000 characters, encoded by Catherine Paul, and markup-reviewed by Kenneth Church. It retains Powell's title page, Introductory Note, contents, medieval Attende lector, and work text through page 324; omits the edition's pages 325-330 word-list and nearly all manuscript marginalia; supplies missing day headings; and restructures chapters 3 and 33.
notes: The current 644,118-byte public raw file is an ETS/EEBO serialization with updated availability wording; the pinned 632,979-byte artifact is DLPSTEXTCLASS. They are not byte- or canonical-XML-identical, but FRONT and BODY alphanumeric text hashes match after stripping whitespace, punctuation, symbols, and markup, and their BODY structures share 9 DIV1, 63 DIV2, 5 DIV3, 384 P, and 327 PB elements.

The current serialization converts the title page to a fourth FRONT DIV1, removes FOREIGN wrappers, changes page-break and note markup, and adjusts whitespace and punctuation boundaries while preserving the textual character sequence under the stated normalization.

entities.label: Lawrence F. Powell's Mirrour of the Blessed Lyf of Jesu Christ (1908)
entities.description: The immediate print source of the U-M encoding. Powell prints the complete Brasenose beta-1 version, including its Attende lector, Love text, Eucharistic treatise, and final prayer, from Brasenose MS 9 with collation against Bodleian MS e. Musaeo 35 and the Sherard manuscript now Beinecke MS 324. Powell's editorial note, contents, and word-list are separate modern edition matter.

bibliographic.citation: Nicholas Love, The Mirrour of the Blessed Lyf of Jesu Christ: A Translation of the Latin Work Entitled Meditationes Vitae Christi, Attributed to Cardinal Bonaventura, Made before the Year 1410, ed. Lawrence F. Powell (Oxford: Clarendon Press; London: Henry Frowde, 1908), iv, 330 pp.
bibliographic.title: The Mirrour of the Blessed Lyf of Jesu Christ

entities.label: PIMS/University of Toronto scan of Powell 1908 at Internet Archive
entities.description: A complete 348-page institutional page-image scan of Powell's 1908 volume, digitized in 2011 and distributed by Internet Archive under identifier mirrourofblessed00bona. The exact item metadata contains no license or rights field.
entities.reproduction_format: Institutional page images distributed as PDF, OCR text, scandata XML, and derivative files by Internet Archive

entities.label: Oxford, Brasenose College MS 9
entities.description: Powell's base witness and Sargent's beta-1 witness Bc, produced in the early fifteenth century on parchment by one scribe. The Mirror includes a table, prohemium beginning at fol. 5r, complete beta-1 text through the explicit and Love colophon at fol. 150v, and a fragmentary Middle English Meditationes de Passione whose leaves were removed and final passage crossed out. That adjacent item is not part of the cataloged Mirror.

holding.notes: Brasenose's current pages say most manuscripts are kept and consulted at the Bodleian, but the exact present request route and custody arrangement for MS 9 were not established from an official item record.

physical_description.extent: ii + 149 + i leaves; flyleaves numbered 1-151, with an unnumbered leaf between 105 and 106
notes: The table is present by fol. 4r, the prohemium begins fol. 5r, and the Mirror explicit and Love colophon are on fol. 150v.

The Geographies description assigns the one scribe the project's Delta designation and a western Buckinghamshire dialect near the Oxfordshire border; this is witness evidence, not a precise place of Love's composition.

entities.label: Oxford, Bodleian Library MS e. Musaeo 35, item 1
entities.description: One of Powell's two collation witnesses and Sargent's alpha-1 witness Mu. The composite parchment manuscript contains the Mirror at pages 1-218, followed by a prayer at pages 219-220 and The Mirror to Lewed Men and Women at pages 221-467. Those adjacent items are outside the cataloged work. Current scholarship dates the Mirror section to about 1420 or the late first quarter of the fifteenth century and associates its production with Margaret Neville and Thomas Beaufort.

date_statements.label: Mirror section production
date_statements.value: About 1420; late first quarter of the fifteenth century

holding.notes: The public scholarly description derives from microfilm and earlier cataloging. The current Bodleian request and copying pages provide the verified access routes.

physical_description.extent: Composite manuscript, vii + 470 pages; the Mirror occupies pages 1-218
notes: The Mirror text is copied by the description's scribe A through page 218.

entities.label: New Haven, Yale University, Beinecke Library MS 324
entities.description: Powell's second collation witness, explicitly identified by the official Beinecke catalog as the Sherard manuscript formerly in Lord Aldenham's collection. Its Mirror table occupies fols. v-vi and text fols. 1r-126v, followed on fol. 126v by the Arundel Memorandum. Several folios are missing, and the scribe deliberately omits Powell pages 308-324; the witness is therefore incomplete relative to Powell's Brasenose text.

date_statements.label: manuscript production
date_statements.value: Second quarter of the fifteenth century; probably London, circa 1430-1450

holding.notes: The official catalog traces ownership from Nicholas Ferrar through James Sherard, Henry Wilson, Lord Aldenham, George Smith, and C. A. Stonehill before Beinecke purchase in 1965.

physical_description.extent: iii + i + 128 + iii leaves; Mirror table fols. v-vi and text fols. 1r-126v
notes: Missing leaves correspond to Powell pages 7-9, 11-20, 214-217, 236-238, and 299-302; Powell pages 308-324 were deliberately omitted.

entities.label: Meditationes vitae Christi (abstract Latin source work)
alternate_labels: Meditations on the Life of Christ
entities.description: The abstract Latin devotional source work from which Love selected, condensed, reordered, translated, and supplemented material. Medieval and Powell framing attributes it to Bonaventure; current scholarship treats it as pseudo-Bonaventuran or anonymous. This entity does not assert use of a particular surviving Latin manuscript or authorship by John of Caulibus.

bibliographic.citation: Meditationes vitae Christi, anonymous pseudo-Bonaventuran Latin devotional work, traditionally attributed to Bonaventure.
bibliographic.title: Meditationes vitae Christi

entities.label: Archived Bodleian sample image of MS e. Musaeo 35, page 80
entities.description: A single legacy Bodleian JPEG of manuscript page 80 recovered through the Internet Archive Wayback Machine. It is partial evidence for the physical witness, not a complete facsimile. No rights statement is embedded in or attached to the archived image.

entities.label: Henry Hucks Gibbs's Catalogue of Books and Manuscripts at St. Dunstan's and Aldenham House (1888)
entities.description: A privately printed collection catalog. Page 200 describes the Sherard manuscript that is now Beinecke MS 324. Page 21 describes a different Mirror manuscript, later Takamiya MS 63, whose supplied portions were copied from the Sherard manuscript; that separate witness is not one of Powell's three and is not added to the direct lineage graph.

bibliographic.citation: Henry Hucks Gibbs, A Catalogue of Some Printed Books and Manuscripts at St. Dunstan's, Regent's Park, and Aldenham House, Herts (London: privately printed, 1888), 200 pp.
bibliographic.title: A Catalogue of Some Printed Books and Manuscripts at St. Dunstan's, Regent's Park, and Aldenham House, Herts

entities.label: Wellcome Library scan of Gibbs 1888 at Internet Archive
entities.description: A complete institutional page-image scan of Gibbs's 1888 catalog distributed by Internet Archive under identifier b24875570. The exact item metadata applies a Creative Commons Public Domain Mark to this catalog scan.
entities.reproduction_format: Institutional page images distributed as PDF, OCR text, scandata XML, and derivative files by Internet Archive

entities.label: Ian Johnson's 2018 study of dialogics in Nicholas Love's Mirror
entities.description: Peer-reviewed supporting scholarship on Love's pre-1410 translation, adaptation, compilation, source voices, anti-Lollard polemic, and Eucharistic additions. It is not an upstream textual source. The catalog-description type is used because the current schema has no scholarly-study entity type.

bibliographic.citation: Ian Johnson, 'Theoretical and Pragmatic Dialogics in and through Nicholas Love's Mirror of the Blessed Life of Jesus Christ,' English 67.257 (2018): 97-118, https://doi.org/10.1093/english/efy022.
bibliographic.title: Theoretical and Pragmatic Dialogics in and through Nicholas Love's Mirror of the Blessed Life of Jesus Christ

rights.component: Nicholas Love's medieval Middle English text and the anonymous Latin source passages as represented in the 1908 edition
rights.basis: The underlying Middle English and Latin works were created in the Middle Ages and their copyright terms have expired.
notes: This status does not transfer to U-M's encoding, modern editorial contributions, scan derivatives, or provider contract terms.

rights.component: Powell's 1908 editorial note, punctuation, collation, word-list, and other original editorial contributions
rights.basis: The edition was published in 1908, before the current United States public-domain cutoff of 1 January 1931 stated by the Copyright Office for works as of 2026.

rights.component: Powell's original literary and editorial contributions, excluding the expired typographical arrangement
rights.basis: UK guidance states that written works usually last for the author's life plus 70 years. Powell died in 1975, so original contributions may remain protected through 2045, subject to facts and exceptions not established here.
notes: This is a cautious status, not a determination of ownership or enforceability.

rights.component: Typographical arrangement of the 1908 published edition
rights.basis: UK guidance gives typographical arrangement of published editions a 25-year term; the 1908 arrangement has expired.

rights.component: Legacy U-M electronic transcription and markup as represented by the pinned DLPSTEXTCLASS artifact and its embedded 1997 availability statement
rights.basis: The XML availability statement permits individual copying for personal use, research, and teaching with retention of the statement and permits linking, but expressly forbids redistribution by commercial or nonprofit content providers. It does not state a copyright license or public-domain status.
notes: The current public raw XML carries a later, materially more permissive public-domain statement. This legacy record preserves the terms actually embedded in the pinned artifact rather than silently replacing them.

rights.component: Current public U-M/Medictionary raw XML, encoded Middle English text, and markup
rights.basis: The current raw XML says the work has been identified as public domain, free of known copyright restrictions including related and neighboring rights, and may be copied, modified, distributed, and performed commercially without permission. The U-M collection page separately identifies the materials as public domain in the United States under No Copyright-United States.
notes: The United States jurisdiction follows the collection-level rights statement. The item's broader wording is preserved in evidence but not generalized here into a worldwide legal conclusion.
The current raw XML and pinned artifact preserve the same normalized alphanumeric FRONT and BODY text but are not identical XML serializations.

rights.component: Exact checked-in XML artifact, including inherited U-M transcription and markup
rights.basis: The CME repository's Unlicense file describes software, while the checked-in XML contains an upstream U-M availability statement with redistribution restrictions. The current U-M raw serialization now carries a public-domain statement, but it is not byte- or canonical-XML-identical to the pinned artifact. The applicability and interaction of the repository license, legacy statement, and current U-M assertion to this exact data file were not resolved.
notes: Repository-level licensing must not be used to erase upstream rights or provider terms without clarification.
This cautious artifact-layer status does not contradict the separate U-M current-distribution assertion recorded for the current raw XML.

rights.component: PIMS/University of Toronto page images and Internet Archive PDF/OCR/scandata derivatives
rights.basis: The exact Internet Archive item metadata names the PIMS/University of Toronto contributor but contains no license or rights field. Public-domain status of the underlying US edition does not by itself establish all rights or provider terms for these derivative files.

rights.component: Medieval textual content of the Mirror witness
rights.basis: The underlying witness text was produced in the early fifteenth century and its copyright term has expired.

rights.component: Any future Brasenose or Bodleian manuscript images or supplied reproductions
rights.basis: No exact public reproduction terms were established for this witness; consultation with the current custodian is required.

rights.component: Medieval textual content of the Mirror witness
rights.basis: The underlying witness text was produced in the fifteenth century and its copyright term has expired.

rights.component: Archived legacy Bodleian JPEG of MS e. Musaeo 35 page 80
rights.basis: The archived sample image has no embedded or attached rights statement. Current Bodleian image-order terms were not applied retroactively to this legacy file.

rights.component: Newly ordered Bodleian scans of out-of-copyright manuscript material
rights.basis: Bodleian's current imaging terms state that scans of out-of-copyright material are supplied under CC BY-NC 4.0; exact application depends on an order and supplied material.
notes: This prospective provider term is not a copyright determination and is not transferred to the archived sample image.

rights.component: Medieval textual content of the Mirror witness and Arundel Memorandum
rights.basis: The underlying witness text was produced in the fifteenth century and its copyright term has expired.

Beinecke states that a reproduction order does not imply publication authorization, that publication images should be ordered through its Digital Studio, and that the library generally does not hold copyright in collection content. Exact terms depend on the requested images and use.
The underlying Latin work was created in the Middle Ages and its copyright term has expired.
The catalog was published in 1888, before the current United States public-domain cutoff.

rights.component: Wellcome Library page images and Internet Archive derivatives of Gibbs 1888
rights.basis: The exact Internet Archive item metadata displays the Creative Commons Public Domain Mark for this scan.
notes: The mark applies to the exact Gibbs catalog scan, not to Powell's scan or any manuscript image.

rights.component: Ian Johnson's accepted author manuscript and scholarly article
rights.basis: The accepted manuscript states copyright in the author in 2018 and says it is made available under the publisher's policies; the repository record does not supply an explicit open-content license.

editorial_practices.source_feature: A beta-1 base text in Brasenose MS 9 and variant evidence from two other witnesses
editorial_practices.representation: Brasenose-based edited text with readings collated from Bodleian MS e. Musaeo 35 and the Sherard manuscript now Beinecke MS 324
editorial_practices.description: Powell reproduces one principal manuscript while using two others for collation; this is not a synoptic or critical edition of all known witnesses.
editorial_practices.scope: The medieval text printed on page [6] and pages 7-324; passage-level dependencies are not fully mapped

editorial_practices.source_feature: Medieval manuscript abbreviations, thorn, yogh, and the abbreviated sacred name ihu
editorial_practices.representation: Abbreviations expanded; thorn printed as th; yogh retained; ihu represented as Jesu
editorial_practices.description: Powell states these character and abbreviation policies in his Introductory Note.

editorial_practices.representation: Modern editorial punctuation supplied by Powell
editorial_practices.description: Powell identifies the punctuation as editorial rather than a diplomatic reproduction of the base manuscript.

editorial_practices.source_feature: A complete 1908 volume with modern title page, Introductory Note, contents, medieval Attende lector and work text, word-list, and manuscript-derived marginalia
editorial_practices.representation: The XML retains the title page, Introductory Note, contents, Attende lector, and medieval text through page 324; omits the pages 325-330 word-list and nearly all manuscript marginalia except chapter numbers
editorial_practices.description: The digital text is a bounded selection from Powell's volume, not an encoding of every edition component or every feature Powell derived from the manuscript.
editorial_practices.scope: Whole digital encoding and its relation to the 1908 volume

editorial_practices.source_feature: Seven day-sections, chapter 3 in three parts, and two chapter-33 headings in Powell's source
editorial_practices.representation: Missing day headers are supplied in brackets; chapter 3 is encoded with three DIV3 parts; the second chapter-33 heading is encoded as a part rather than a duplicate chapter
editorial_practices.description: U-M records its supplied headings and explicit restructuring of chapters 3 and 33 in the encoding declaration.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Love's rhyming Ave Maria meditation at the end of chapter 3, with an additional beta-1 'Gete us' rhyme
editorial_practices.representation: Encoded inside prose P elements without LG/L lineation
editorial_practices.description: The XML's all-P representation erases the verse grouping and lineation identified by textual scholarship; it must not be used as evidence that chapter 3 is prose-only.
notes: One verse group is defensible, but this manifest does not impose a verse-line count because Powell and the XML run the passage as prose.

evidence.citation: General Corpus, CME/source/CME_phase_1-2/LoveMirrour.xml, pinned CME commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.locator: HEADER; FRONT nodes LoveMirrour:1-LoveMirrour:3; BODY nodes LoveMirrour:4-LoveMirrour:12
evidence.summary: Primary evidence for the exact repository artifact, U-M source and encoding statements, 1997 publication, availability terms, Powell edition, Brasenose source designation, retained and omitted features, structural changes, embedded Latin, Attende lector, Love colophon, and encoded work boundary.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library Digital Collections, LoveMirrour named-item record and Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse collection.
evidence.summary: Official public route for the U-M electronic text; browser access is public, the collection advertises XML downloads, and automated retrieval of the Quod interface may encounter provider bot protection.

evidence.citation: Current public raw XML for University of Michigan LoveMirrour, distributed through Medictionary.
evidence.locator: ETS/HEADER availability and source metadata; ETS/EEBO/TEXT/FRONT and BODY
evidence.summary: The current 644,118-byte ETS/EEBO XML is structurally complete and carries updated public-domain availability wording. It is not byte- or canonical-XML-identical to the pinned 632,979-byte DLPSTEXTCLASS file: the wrapper, title-page structure, FOREIGN, NOTE, PB, whitespace, and punctuation markup differ. Direct comparison nevertheless finds matching normalized alphanumeric FRONT SHA-256 4fe74a5ecd607957b98cc816d85a62813430d44135dca0e9055326d8ce88be09 and BODY SHA-256 97ce70db27b7cc0b893601e73b6b18b4c09da0751604c2d5b2da506bc5d2a690, plus identical BODY counts of 9 DIV1, 63 DIV2, 5 DIV3, 384 P, and 327 PB elements.
notes: The raw XML references a remote EEBO-profile DTD that was unavailable during local validation, but the file is well-formed and parses without fetching the DTD.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library Digital Collections, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse collection page.
evidence.locator: collection description and Rights and Permissions
evidence.summary: Describes the corpus as manually keyed from public-domain editions with in-house proofreading and basic TEI markup, says all texts are available for bulk XML download, and labels the materials public domain in the United States under No Copyright-United States.

evidence.citation: Nicholas Love, The Mirrour of the Blessed Lyf of Jesu Christ, ed. Lawrence F. Powell (Oxford: Clarendon Press; London: Henry Frowde, 1908), PIMS/University of Toronto scan at Internet Archive.
evidence.locator: title page; Introductory Note pp. iii-iv; contents pp. [1]-[6]; Attende lector p. [6]; work pp. 7-324; word-list pp. 325-330
evidence.summary: Complete scan of the immediate edition, confirming its title, Brasenose base, two collated witnesses, selected medieval and modern components, editorial practices, Powell/Brasenose beta-1 work boundary, and the word-list omitted from U-M's XML.

evidence.citation: Visual inspection of the cached PIMS/University of Toronto Powell 1908 PDF.
evidence.locator: 348 PDF pages; scan leaves 7-16 for retained front matter; scan leaf 16 Attende lector; scan leaves 17-334 / printed pp. 7-324 for the cataloged work; scan leaves 335-340 / printed pp. 325-330 for the omitted word-list
evidence.summary: Confirms that the PDF is a complete readable volume, maps the corpus work's start and end, separates the medieval Attende lector from Powell's editorial front, and verifies that U-M stops before Powell's word-list.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata API response for mirrourofblessed00bona.
evidence.locator: metadata.identifier, contributor, collection, scanner, operator, publicdate, licenseurl, rights
evidence.summary: Identifies the PIMS/University of Toronto contribution, Toronto scanning, and 7 September 2011 public date. The exact item has no licenseurl or rights value.

evidence.citation: Ryan Perry, description of Oxford, Brasenose College MS 9, Geographies of Orthodoxy, rev. 1 June 2010.
evidence.locator: contents, physical description, provenance, incipit, explicit, colophon, scribal description, and textual affiliation
evidence.summary: Identifies current Brasenose MS 9 with Powell's e.9, the early-fifteenth-century parchment witness, table and prohemium locations, explicit and Love colophon at fol. 150v, one scribe, beta-1 affiliation and Bc siglum, and the adjacent fragmentary Meditationes de Passione material.

evidence.citation: Brasenose College Library, 'Contact and Access Information' and 'Antiquarian Books and Manuscripts.'
evidence.summary: Provides the special-collections contact and appointment route and states that most Brasenose manuscripts are kept and consulted at the Bodleian; it does not provide an exact public item record or reproduction terms for MS 9.

evidence.citation: Geographies of Orthodoxy description of Oxford, Bodleian Library MS e. Musaeo 35, based on microfilm and scholarship.
evidence.locator: contents, production date, material, extent, scribes, provenance, and textual affiliation
evidence.summary: Identifies the composite manuscript, Mirror at pages 1-218, adjacent prayer and Mirror to Lewed Men and Women, parchment support, c. 1420 dating, first-item scribe, alpha-1 affiliation and Mu siglum, and Margaret Neville/Thomas Beaufort association.

evidence.citation: Archived Bodleian Summary Catalogue page image for MS e. Musaeo 35.
evidence.locator: legacy Summary Catalogue page
evidence.summary: Cached metadata-only legacy catalog-page image supporting the Bodleian shelfmark and description. It is not a manuscript facsimile.

evidence.citation: Archived legacy Bodleian sample image of MS e. Musaeo 35, page 80.
evidence.locator: manuscript page 80 only
evidence.summary: One public legacy sample image of the Mirror witness; partial coverage only and no embedded or attached rights statement.

evidence.citation: Visual and file-format inspection of the cached Bodleian MS e. Musaeo 35 page-80 sample.
evidence.locator: 599 x 900 JPEG, one manuscript page
evidence.summary: Confirms the file is a single readable manuscript-page JPEG rather than a complete image set; no embedded rights metadata was found.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Special Collections request, remote copying, and Imaging Services ordering pages.
evidence.summary: Current official routes for requesting physical special-collections material and ordering new reproductions, subject to registration, feasibility, fees, and provider terms.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries Imaging Services, copyright and reproducing images guidance and current image-order terms.
evidence.summary: States the distinction between copyright and permission and gives current CC BY-NC 4.0 terms for newly supplied scans of out-of-copyright material. Those terms were treated as prospective and not retroactively applied to the archived legacy sample.

evidence.citation: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Pre-1600 Manuscripts, MS 324.
evidence.locator: contents, physical description, collation, date and place, provenance, and bibliography
evidence.summary: Official exact-item record identifying the manuscript as Powell's Sherard/Lord Aldenham witness, giving table and text folios, Arundel Memorandum, lacunae, deliberate omission, production date and probable place, physical extent, and provenance through Beinecke acquisition.

evidence.citation: Beinecke Library, 'How to Request Items,' 'Reproduction Orders,' and digital photography policies.
evidence.summary: Provides the current onsite request, researcher photography, and reproduction-order routes; reproduction orders are reviewed and do not imply publication authorization.

evidence.citation: Beinecke Library, 'Copyright Questions.'
evidence.summary: States that Beinecke generally does not hold copyrights in collection content, welcomes uses of public-domain material, and places responsibility for copyright analysis on the user; provider reproduction processes remain separate.

evidence.citation: Henry Hucks Gibbs, A Catalogue of Some Printed Books and Manuscripts at St. Dunstan's, Regent's Park, and Aldenham House, Herts (1888), Wellcome Library scan at Internet Archive.
evidence.locator: printed page 21 and Addenda printed page 200
evidence.summary: Page 200 describes the Sherard manuscript now Beinecke MS 324, matching its 128-leaf extent, lacunae, truncation, and provenance. Page 21 describes a different Aldenham Mirror manuscript now Takamiya MS 63 and prevents conflation of the two.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata API response for b24875570.
evidence.locator: metadata.identifier, contributor, publicdate, and rights
evidence.summary: Identifies the Wellcome Library contribution, 4 July 2016 public date, and exact-item Creative Commons Public Domain Mark assertion.

evidence.citation: Ian Johnson, 'Theoretical and Pragmatic Dialogics in and through Nicholas Love's Mirror of the Blessed Life of Jesus Christ,' English 67.257 (2018): 97-118, accepted author manuscript.
evidence.locator: abstract and discussions of pre-1410 production, pseudo-Bonaventuran source, translation, adaptation, compilation, anti-Lollard polemic, and Eucharistic additions
evidence.summary: Peer-reviewed scholarship supporting the source-work boundary and Love's creative transformation of the Latin source. The cached 32-page accepted manuscript is complete; qpdf reports recoverable zero-offset warnings but succeeds and all pages extract.

evidence.citation: Copyright and repository-policy statement in Ian Johnson's cached 2018 accepted author manuscript.
evidence.locator: cover page copyright and availability notice
evidence.summary: The manuscript states copyright in the author in 2018 and availability under publisher policy, without an explicit Creative Commons or other open-content license.

evidence.citation: Allan Westphall and David Falls, 'Mirror of the Blessed Life of Jesus Christ,' Geographies of Orthodoxy textual profile, rev. 1 June 2010.
evidence.locator: date, sources, provenance, contents, textual authority, and theology
evidence.summary: Scholarly profile grounded in Sargent's critical edition, supporting the pseudo-Bonaventuran source, Love's transformations, sixty-four-chapter and seven-day organization, marginal N/B apparatus, Eucharistic treatise, anti-Lollard interventions, beta/alpha revision caution, and only a proposed rather than proven physical Latin exemplar.

evidence.citation: Geographies of Orthodoxy, 'The textual affiliations of the witnesses to the Mirror' and 'The Meditation on the Ave Maria,' based on Michael G. Sargent's critical edition.
evidence.locator: beta, alpha, and gamma affiliations; The Meditation on the Ave Maria
evidence.summary: Explains the beta form as the likely earlier authorial draft and alpha as revision, and prints Love's rhyming chapter-3 Ave Maria meditation with the additional beta-1 'Gete us' rhyme. It grounds a mixed chapter and one verse group but not stable Powell/XML lineation.

evidence.citation: Availability statement in the U-M LoveMirrour XML header.
evidence.locator: HEADER/FILEDESC/PUBLICATIONSTMT/AVAILABILITY
evidence.summary: Permits copying by individuals for personal use, research, and teaching if the statement remains; permits linking; expressly forbids redistribution by commercial and nonprofit content providers. It does not grant an open copyright license.

evidence.citation: Kyvero Press CME repository LICENSE file.
evidence.locator: Unlicense text and opening reference to software
evidence.summary: The repository license dedicates software to the public domain, but its application to inherited U-M XML data with an embedded availability statement is ambiguous and was not assumed.

evidence.citation: United States Copyright Office, 'What is Copyright?', public-domain guidance current in 2026.
evidence.locator: public-domain section
evidence.summary: States that works published before 1 January 1931 are in the public domain as of 2026, supporting the US status of Powell 1908 and Gibbs 1888; the medieval texts independently far predate modern terms.

evidence.citation: Government of the United Kingdom, 'How copyright protects your work: How long copyright lasts.'
evidence.locator: written works and typographical arrangement terms
evidence.summary: States that written works usually last for life plus 70 years and typographical arrangements for 25 years, supporting a cautious UK distinction between Powell's original editorial contributions and the expired 1908 layout.

open_questions.question: What is the exact current custody and request path for Brasenose College MS 9?
next_steps: Contact Brasenose Library at library@bnc.ox.ac.uk with the current and historical shelfmarks.
Ask whether consultation and reproduction requests should be placed directly through the Bodleian and request an official exact-item record if one exists.
notes: Powell's 1908 statement that the manuscript was deposited at the Bodleian is historical evidence, not confirmation of today's operational route.

open_questions.question: Can complete public facsimiles be established for Brasenose MS 9, Bodleian MS e. Musaeo 35, or Beinecke MS 324?
next_steps: Use the recorded consultation and reproduction-order routes if full page images are required for passage-level work.
notes: The archived Bodleian page-80 JPEG is explicitly partial and must not be described as a complete facsimile.

open_questions.question: Which exact Powell readings derive from Bodleian MS e. Musaeo 35 and Beinecke MS 324?
next_steps: Map Powell's notes and variants against the three witnesses or Sargent's critical apparatus before assigning passage-level ancestry.
Keep the present collation relations at selected-passage scope until that work is complete.

open_questions.question: Which physical Latin Meditationes vitae Christi exemplar or exemplars did Nicholas Love use?
next_steps: Evaluate the proposed Ripon Cathedral MS 6 connection and reading-level comparisons in current scholarship.
Retain only the abstract Latin source-work relation until physical use is established.

open_questions.question: How do the legacy U-M redistribution restrictions, current U-M public-domain assertion, and CME Unlicense apply to the exact pinned General Corpus artifact?
next_steps: Ask U-M whether the current public-domain assertion supersedes the legacy availability statement for earlier DLPSTEXTCLASS serializations as well as the current ETS/EEBO file.
Ask the CME repository maintainers whether the Unlicense was intended to cover inherited XML data, and preserve the embedded legacy statement meanwhile.

open_questions.question: What is the exact UK copyright and ownership status of Powell's original 1908 editorial contributions?
next_steps: Confirm any publication agreements, assignments, and applicable UK transitional provisions.
Treat original Powell contributions as potentially protected in the UK through 2045 unless qualified advice establishes otherwise.
notes: The typographical arrangement has a separate expired term and is not the unresolved component.

open_questions.question: How should the chronology and exact contents of Love's beta and alpha authorial forms be dated and related at passage level?
next_steps: Consult Sargent's full critical introduction and apparatus before assigning individual additions or the Eucharistic treatise to a precise revision event.
Continue qualifying Powell's Brasenose text as beta-1 rather than a universal final text.
